title: A Hat for Mistress Mouse
summary: In her busy workshop, Hattie the Mouse receives a specific request from Mistress Mouse for a unique cardboard hat featuring green stripes, pretend red mushrooms, and colorful beads. Hattie carefully measures and assembles the pieces, dealing with frustration and small mistakes along the way by practicing deep breathing. After the hat is baked, painted, and decorated, Mistress Mouse proudly visits her mother, Mischief, to show off the finished masterpiece, and the two enjoy a peaceful meal of corn together.
audioFilename: Stars and stories - A Hat for Mistress Mouse.m4a

highlightQuote.text: But don't be really mad Hattie. All of your feelings are okay.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Stars and stories... no, you mean stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No stars!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hey, do you want to tell a story this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: About Hattie maybe?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: How about a story about Oscar the robot?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I don't like a story about Oscar the robot, I like stories about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Of course you do. So who's going to visit Hattie this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A robot maybe? Or not a robot? No robot. I'm going to come to the workshop right now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who's going to come?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mr. Mouse. But stories are going to be Mistress Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And I like to do a story about Hattie and someone knocking at the door.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was in her workshop working on a hat and she heard someone knocking at the door.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And it was Mistress.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was who?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mistress.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was Mistress Mouse. And so Hattie opened the door and said, 'Oh, hello Mistress Mouse. What brings you to my workshop today?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And what did she say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I would like a hat for me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you would like a hat? Well, you've come to the right place. What kind of a hat would you like, Mistress Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I would like a big hat with medium stripes and small stripes, stripes and lots of mushrooms to go on that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now let me make sure I've got this straight. You'd like a big hat with green stripes did you say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And and also little stripes. And mushrooms to go on it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. And are the mushrooms on top?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Are they big or small?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They're a little bit like medium, but not like cheese. Yeah, that means they're small. I mean the mushrooms are small.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And are they real mushrooms or pictures of mushrooms?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They are pretend mushrooms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, pretend mushrooms on top. Hmm. And what color are they?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They are red.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Red mushrooms on... wow, that sounds like a beautiful hat. Well, it's going to take me some time to make this hat.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and then you could bake it in your oven.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, I could bake it in my oven. Now, you know what? I don't happen to have any pretend mushrooms right here in my workshop.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But they're shown in your drawer.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they're in my drawer? Hmm, let me go check. Oh yeah. Look at this, there's a drawer that says on it pretend mushrooms. Let me open it up. Zzzz. Oh, look at that. It's empty. I must have used up all my pretend mushrooms.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But you did not. You saved some red mushrooms for later, you know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I did? Oh, look at that. Huh. Right here tucked into the back of the drawer. I missed them at first. There's three perfect small red pretend mushrooms.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and you have to glue them on.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And I have to glue them on. Oh, can you find my glue for me, Mistress Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. And right now I see one of your nice glues, your purple glue.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, my purple glue. Yes, that's a very good kind of glue for gluing mushrooms onto a hat. But I have to make the rest of the hat first. Hmm.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But first you have to draw the stripe.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, I need some material for the hat. Do you think I should make it out of cloth or wood or fur?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm, maybe cardboard.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, I have plenty of cardboard. Okay. Now let me just measure your head really fast. Sh-sh-sh. And Hattie the Mouse took out her measuring tape and measured around Mistress Mouse's head. And she said, 'Aha, okay, I've got your measurements. Now I'll just trace them out on the cardboard and then add a little piece here and then get another piece to cut out to go over the top and another piece to go around the sides.' And she was drawing all the patterns on the cardboard. And then she said, 'Oh, where are my scissors? Do you see them anywhere, Mistress Mouse?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I do see a pair of red scissors and I see a pair of yellow scissors and I see a pair of orange scissors and I see a pair of red scissors and I see a pair of green scissors and I see a pair of blue scissors and I see a pair of purple scissors. Which red pair of scissors would you like?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I think I'd like to use the yellow scissors for this because those are good for cutting cardboard. Could you pass them to me, please?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pass, pass.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thank you. Okay. And then snip, snip, cut, cut, crunch, crunch, crunch, crunch, snip, snip, cut, cut. Hattie cut the cardboard along all the lines that she had drawn on it to make the pieces of the hat. And now she had to assemble them. That means putting them all together. And now I need my glue. Oh yes, here's that purple glue you found for me. Glue, glue, stick, stick, stick. And so she stuck all the pieces of the hat together. But then wait a minute. Pop! Part of the hat popped right off. And Hattie said, 'Oh no, I guess I didn't do it right. Even though I'm really good at making hats, even I sometimes make a mistake.' And it's okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I can help you find what you're missing. Hattie, why are you missing?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thanks for the help, Mistress Mouse. Well, I just didn't put it together quite right and now I'm feeling kind of frustrated.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, I have something different you could do. Maybe you could stick one of these beads on.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a bead? But I'm so upset I can't work on the hat because I'm really mad because it fell apart.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But don't be really mad Hattie. All of your feelings are okay.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're right. All my feelings are okay. But what can I do to help me feel less mad?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You could take a deep breath. Then count to four.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: One, two, three, four. Oh yeah. Now I do feel a little bit better. That was good advice.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And you can blow these lines instead.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hey, look at those beautiful beads you found. I think that's a good idea. All right, now that I feel a little bit better, I think I'm going to glue the hat back together. And now I see...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and you can add mushrooms together too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yep, I'll add the mushrooms at the end. I see what I did wrong the first time and now I'm going to glue it together really good so it doesn't pop open. And now oh, I just need to get some paint to paint the stripes on. Swish-swish-swish-swish-swish-swish. And we've got to wait for that to dry.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wait for a few minutes. And what did Hattie the Mouse and Mistress Mouse do while they were waiting for the paint to dry?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then they baked it and that was ready for Mistress.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then it was ready. But oh wait, it's missing some things. It's missing the mushrooms on top. Where did I leave those? Oh yeah. And she glued the mushrooms right on top. So it was a beautiful green striped cardboard hat with bright red pretend mushrooms on top. And she said, 'Oh Mistress, do you still want me to glue these beads on?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I do.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. I'll add some beads. And so she glued four different colored beads onto different parts of the hat. And then she said, 'Huh.' And she turned it all around and she said, 'I think it's done. We have one last thing to do though.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We have to try it on you and make sure it fits. Come on over here, Mistress Mouse. And Hattie Mouse lifted up the beautiful new hat and put it on Mistress Mouse's head. And she said, 'Okay, does that feel good? Does it fit well?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mistress said, 'It fits me well.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh good. Well then, I think we're all done. Congratulations, you have a beautiful new hat. And what did Mistress Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Thank you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're welcome. Now that you've got your beautiful new hat, where do you think you're going to go to wear it to? Who are you going to show it to?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: To Mom Mistress.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh wait, who's Mistress Mouse's mother?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Mistress's mother, her name is Mischief.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then Mistress came to her house and then she said 'Mommy, mommy, look at my new hat!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mistress Mouse said, 'Wow, what a beautiful hat! Would you like to come in to have a meal together?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, come on in and what did they have for their meal?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Corn.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They had some delicious corn together. And that was the end of the story. Because now it's time to turn off the stars.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Mistress Mouse
characters.description: A polite three-year-old mouse and resourceful shopkeeper, this community cornerstone balances a bold artistic spirit with sharp mechanical grit. Whether crafting intricate Lego creations in her sunroom or piloting her signature pink tow truck to solve puzzles and assist neighbors, she is a master of the road and a multi-talented creator. From famously adorning cars with blue stripes to dominating the race track, her inventive soul is always on the move. Usually seen in a mushroom-adorned cardboard hat with an orange sparkle purse, this resourceful driver remains an essential, evocative fixture of every neighborhood adventure.

characters.name: Oscar the Robot
characters.description: A character suggested for a story but ultimately rejected in favor of Hattie.

characters.name: Mischief
characters.description: Proprietor of the analog-scented tape store on the corner of Poplar and Popcornioni, this avian known as Velociraptorning is Hattie’s loyal companion and the devoted mother of Mistress Mouse. Whether aiding Oscar on his adventures from their shared burrow or tending her wall of magnetic spools, she remains a soul of simple pleasures—sketching in sun-drenched parks, sharing meals of corn, and admiring her daughter’s new hat with a prideful, artistic eye.

places.name: The Workshop
places.description: Hattie the Mouse's workplace, equipped with a wide variety of colored scissors, glue, and a baking oven.

places.name: The Drawer
places.description: A storage space in the workshop where Hattie keeps her 'pretend mushrooms'.

places.name: Mistress's House
places.description: A cozy residence where Mistress Mouse and her mother, Mischief, share celebratory feasts of golden corn, featuring a light-drenched sunroom where the mouse friends gather to scour for hidden clues.
